text: Fill in the blanks on cardiovascular physiology
questions: The SA node fires at an intrinsic rate of *70-80/70 to 80* beats per minute and is the primary pacemaker of the heart.
The property of *automaticity* allows cardiac muscle to generate action potentials spontaneously without neural input.
The long refractory period (~250 ms) of cardiac muscle prevents *tetanus/tetanic contraction* — ensuring the heart relaxes between beats.
Cardiac output is defined as the product of *stroke volume* and heart rate.
Normal resting cardiac output is approximately *5/five* litres per minute.
The *Frank-Starling/Starling* law states that the force of contraction increases with increasing venous return (preload).
The first heart sound (S1) is produced by the closure of the *atrioventricular/AV* valves (mitral and tricuspid).
Short-term blood pressure regulation is primarily mediated by *baroreceptors* located in the carotid sinus and aortic arch.
The *Fick* principle states that cardiac output equals oxygen consumption divided by the arteriovenous oxygen difference.
Gap junctions between cardiac myocytes allow the heart to function as a *syncytium/functional syncytium*, enabling coordinated contraction.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review the material and try again.
Good effort! Review the areas you missed.
Excellent! You have a strong grasp of this topic.
